title: About the National Occupation List | Stats NZ
content:
## About the National Occupation List
20 November 2024, 2:00pm
21 May 2026 : We have updated the summary lists for the National Occupation List V2.0.0 and V3.0.0.
See National Occupation List updates for summary files.
14 October 2025 : We have updated the summary of changes between the Australia and New Zealand Standard Classification of Occupation V1.3.0 (ANZSCO) and National Occupation List V1.0.0 (NOL).
See National Occupation List updates for summary files.
8 August 2025 : We have updated the summary lists for the National Occupation List V1.0.0 and V2.0.0.
See National Occupation List updates for summary files.
Use this page to submit a proposal for making changes to current occupation classifications or propose a new occupation.
The National Occupation List (NOL) is an independent New Zealand-focused occupation classification.
Comparable but tailored occupational classifications for Australia and New Zealand , published by the Australian Bureau of Statistics (ABS) and Stats NZ on 8 October 2024, indicates that while maintaining comparability with ABS, we have introduced our own tailored occupational statistical classifications.
The NOL enables us to add new and emerging occupations to meet user demand and need, and better reflect New Zealand's labour market and economy. Maintaining alignment with Australia and internationally will ensure comparability across the New Zealand, Australian, and international labour markets and economies.
As custodian of the NOL, we are responsible for the ongoing maintenance of the NOL, and for managing the stakeholder and user expectations for keeping the list current.
We also maintain the list in Ariā (the platform for classifications, concepts, and concordances).
The current version of the NOL, which contains changes after the most recent consultation, and concordances to the Australian and New Zealand standard classification of Occupation (ANZSCO) V1.3 and the International Standard classification of Occupation 2008 (ISCO08) V1.0 are available from Aria.

## National Occupation List and concordances from Aria
- National Occupation List V3.0 (released 1 January 2026) ⁠
- National Occupation List V2.0 to V3.0 concordance ⁠
- National Occupation List V3.0 to ANZSCO V1.3 V1.0.0 concordance
- National Occupation List V1.0 to ISCO08 V1.0 V1.0.0 , concordance

## National Occupation List updates
- Summary of changes between National Occupation List V2.0.0 and V3.0.0 (NOL) , released May 2026, (published 1 January 2026)
- Summary of changes between National Occupation List V1.0.0 and V2.0.0 (NOL) , released July 2025, (published 8 August 2025)
- Summary of changes between Australia and New Zealand Standard Classification of Occupation V1.3.0 (ANZSCO) and National Occupation List V1.0.0 (NOL) , November 2024, (published 8 August 2025)

## Updating the National Occupation List
We invite stakeholders and users to submit proposals for change to existing content, for example, updating occupation titles, definitions, or skill levels; or submit proposals for new content for the list at any time to Stats NZ.
The form allows you to submit up to 10 proposed changes and 10 new occupations.
If you have more than 10 changes or additions, either submit another form, or download the Excel file below, and send your proposals to occupations@stats.govt.nz .

## Download Excel version of the proposal form
- Proposal form for changes to the National Occupation List Excel spreadsheet, 59 KB

### Proposal form for changes to the National Occupation List
Excel spreadsheet, 59 KB
Questions in the form include details about your proposed changes and the rationale:
- the change you are proposing, is it a change to an existing occupation, title, skill level or definition, or the addition of a new occupation
- the proposed new occupation and provide a title, definition text, and the appropriate NZQCF level and qualification
- the changes proposed to an existing occupation such as title, definition or skill level
- the rationale for the change being proposed: this could include any policy, data, information, workforce planning/development, or skill shortage needs.

## Future updates to the National Occupation List
We plan to publish regular annual updates from 31 March 2027 .
For occupations to be considered for the annual updates they need to be received before 31 October the previous year.
We will inform users of proposed changes prior to the annual release. We will work collaboratively with users using an iterative and dynamic approach to content change.
We may adjust the frequency of the release of content depending on the number of proposals received.

## Privacy implications
Privacy statement for stats.govt.nz ensures all information you provide will be kept confidential. Your information will be used by the Stats NZ Standards team to review updates to the National Occupation List. The occupation title received in the proposal will be shared with the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) for the purposes of sharing potential new content for their Tahatū platform. No other information received in a proposal will be shared.

## Criteria for accepting your proposal
Once we receive your form, we review the information for inclusion in the list and check for duplications, consistency in terminology, and any other aspect that might need to be addressed before accepting the change.  We also engage with other experts to verify the information provided.
We inform all users of proposed changes before any annual release and work collaboratively with users, using an iterative and dynamic approach to content change.
occupations@stats.govt.nz
- Comparable but tailored occupational classifications for Australia and New Zealand 08 October 2024 Image: Stats ABS logo Lockup 01
- The future of occupation classifications in Aotearoa New Zealand: Findings from consultation 15 July to 9 August 2024 08 October 2024
